reason: The text explicitly contains both elements of the statement. In Exodus 14:14, Moses tells the people 'יְהוָה יִלָּחֵם לָכֶם וְאַתֶּם תַּחֲרִישׁוּן' (The LORD will fight for you, and you shall hold your peace/be silent). Then in Exodus 14:15, God commands Moses to tell the children of Israel 'וְיִסָּעוּ' (to go forward/proceed). The passage thus contains a clear call for the people to be silent/shut up and to move forward, which corresponds directly to the statement's characterization.
